title: TIFF Image
documentation:
## Purpose
Represents a TIFF (Tagged Image File Format) raster image. TIFF is a flexible, high-quality format widely used in professional photography, printing, scanning, and document imaging pipelines.

## When to use
Use `media:tiff;image` when a node produces or consumes TIFF images, particularly in archival, print, or scanner workflows where lossless quality and rich metadata (EXIF, IPTC) must be preserved. For web delivery or general-purpose use, prefer [PNG Image](https://capdag.com/browse/media:png) or [JPEG Image](https://capdag.com/browse/media:jpeg;image).

## Notes
TIFF supports multiple compression schemes (LZW, ZIP, JPEG, uncompressed) and can store multiple pages in a single file. Consumers should handle multi-page TIFF files and confirm the compression type before processing.
